headline: Credit Card Charges Show This Glaring Divergence With The Economy
summary: The country's largest credit card companies saw first-quarter spending increase 7% year over year to $1.1 trillion.

headline: Warren Buffett Has Beaten the Market Over a 60-Year Stretch. Here Are 3 of the Billionaire's Favorite Stocks.

headline: Can AmEx's Canada Dining Expansion Drive Higher Card Spend?
summary: AXP expands card acceptance at three Canadian restaurant chains as AmEx targets higher dining spend and deeper cardmember engagement.

headline: Klarna Q1 Earnings Beat Estimates on Strong GMV & Network Expansion
summary: KLAR tops Q1 estimates as revenues jump 44% to $1.01B, GMV hit $33.7B, and its consumer and merchant network expands sharply.

headline: Meredith Whitney raises alarms on what the rise of shadow banking says about consumers
summary: Meredith Whitney, Meredith Whitney Advisory Group CEO, joins 'Money Movers' to discuss concerns about the U.S. consumer, the rise of shadow banking, what companies are signaling about their consumers, and more.

raw.headline: Meredith Whitney raises alarms on what the rise of shadow banking says about consumers

headline: Lowe's Pro Card Launch Aims To Deepen Rewards And Undervaluation Story
summary: Lowe's Companies (NYSE:LOW) has launched the MyLowe's Pro Rewards American Express Card in partnership with Synchrony and American Express. The new co-branded card gives Pro customers rewards on purchases both at Lowe's locations and with other merchants that accept American Express. The product is aimed at expanding payment flexibility and rewards options for business customers who shop regularly for home improvement supplies. Lowe's, trading at around $220.45, is putting more focus on its...

headline: 3 Market-Beating Stocks Worth Your Attention
summary: The best-performing stocks typically have robust sales growth, increasing margins, and rising returns on capital, and those that can maintain this trifecta year in and year out often become the legends of the investing world.
